en: the type of sensory neurons called polymodal nociceptors.
ko: 다중모달 통각수용기라는 감각 뉴런을 활성화시키는 것입니다.

en: You have these all over your body, including your mouth and nose.
ko: 이것은 입과 코를 포함해 온몸에 있습니다.

en: And they're the same receptors that are activated by extreme heat.
ko: 그리고 이것은 극심한 열에 의해 활성화되는 동일한 수용체입니다.

en: So when you eat a chili pepper, your mouth feels like it's burning, because your brain actually thinks it's burning.
ko: 그래서 고추를 먹으면 입이 타는 것처럼 느껴지는데

en: The opposite happens when you eat something with menthol in it.
ko: 멘톨이 들어간 음식을 먹으면 반대 현상이 일어납니다.

en: The cool, minty compound is activating your cold receptors.
ko: 시원한 민트 화합물이 냉각 수용체를 활성화시킵니다.

en: When these heat-sensitive receptors are activated, your body thinks it's in contact with a
dangerous heat source and reacts accordingly. This is why you start to sweat and your heart starts beating faster.
The peppers have elicited the same fight-or-flight response with which your body reacts to most threats.
ko: 고추가 대부분의 위협에 대해 몸이 반응하는

en: But you may have noticed that not all spicy foods are spicy in the same way.
ko: 하지만 모든 매운 음식이 같은 방식으로 맵지는 않다는 걸 눈치챘을 겁니다.

en: And the difference lies in the types of compounds involved.
ko: 그 차이는 관련된 화합물의 종류에 있습니다.

en: The capsaicin and piperine found in black pepper and chili peppers are made up of larger,
heavier molecules called alkyl amides. And those mostly stay in your mouth.
Mustard, horseradish, and wasabi are made up of smaller molecules called isothiocyanates
ko: 겨자, 고추냉이, 와사비는 더 작은 분자로 구성되어 있습니다.
